methodology: Cross-reference network analysis (136-vote connection — absolute record in entire dataset across all queries) + patristic commentary reading (10+ commentators representing 6 distinct theological schools: conditional foreknowledge, grace-will integration, corporate election, anti-fatalism, Calvinism, Arminianism) + CCEL confessional documents (Canons of Dort, Westminster, Belgic Confession) + topic verification (ELECTION 53 aspects, PREDESTINATION 80 refs)

evidence: commentaries (Diodorus, Augustine, Haydock, Symeon, Cyril — 10 entries)
commentaries (Jerome 'election FOR holiness', Chrysostom, Ambrosiaster, John of Damascus — 12 entries)
commentaries (Ambrosiaster 'foreknowledge of future merit', Gennadius, Augustine — 5 entries)
commentaries (Haydock 'three divine Persons conspire in salvation')

relevance_change.reason: Uses 'foreknowledge' (πρόγνωσις) explicitly — the only gold ref that names the concept directly. Haydock integrates all three Persons of the Trinity in the election process: Father's foreknowledge, Spirit's sanctification, Son's blood. 7 votes to ROM.11.2.

new_refs_added.justification: Already in gold_references but MISSING from relevance_judgments. 45 votes para PHP.1.6. 14 votes para EPH.1.4. The 'golden chain': predestined → called → justified → glorified. Augustine: 'one cannot be justified without being called.' Inseparable from 8:29.

73 votes para ROM.8.29. 12 votes de EPH.1.5. Uses 'predestined' explicitly: 'predestined according to the purpose of him who works all things according to the counsel of his will.' Third pillar of predestination in Ephesians.

6 votes para ROM.9.18. 3 votes para 2TH.2.13 and EPH.2.8-9. Key verse on divine sovereignty: 'So then it depends not on human will or exertion, but on God, who has mercy.' Complements Rm 9:11 (Jacob/Esau) with the principle.

candidates_noted.justification: 6 votes para ROM.8.28. 5 votes para ROM.8.29-30. Only NARRATIVE verse (not epistolary) on predestination: 'as many as were appointed to eternal life believed.'
58 votes para LUK.11.9. Words of Jesus on election — gospel angle different from Pauline. 'You did not choose me, but I chose you.'
6 votes para ROM.9.27. Connects OT election (Israel) → NT (remnant by grace). Topic ELECTION.

absolute_record_connection.note: ABSOLUTE RECORD in the entire bible-crossrefs-dataset (1.2M links) across ALL 10 extreme queries validated. Confirms that Romans 8:29 and Ephesians 1:4-5 express the same Pauline concept of predestination in two different letters.

six_theological_schools.description: This query uniquely reveals 6 distinct theological positions in the commentaries

schools.name: Conditional Foreknowledge
schools.formula: God foresees who will believe → predestines those believers → provides grace

schools.name: Grace-Will Integration
schools.formula: Grace and free will cooperate without causal priority. Grace sufficient but not coercive.

schools.name: Corporate Election
schools.formula: God elected the CHURCH as body, not unconditional individuals

schools.name: Anti-Fatalism (Eastern Orthodox)
schools.formula: Foreknowledge ≠ causation. 'Zeal and deliberate choice of each effects the victory.'

schools.name: Calvinism
schools.formula: Unconditional election, irresistible grace. 'Foreknowledge is not the mother of election.'

schools.name: Arminianism
schools.formula: Conditional election based on foreseen faith. Prevenient grace universal.

six_theological_schools.significance_for_tcc: The hybrid search must retrieve verses supporting ALL sides of this debate. A system biased toward any single theological tradition would miss relevant results. This query tests theological neutrality of the retrieval system.

confessional_documents_in_ccel.description: Three major confessional documents in CCEL treat predestination as a central article

golden_chain.augustine_note: 'Many are called but few are chosen. But since the elect have certainly been called, it is obvious that they have not been justified without being called.' — establishes logical ordering of the chain
